risk_if_not_implemented: Without Use of Privileged Utility Programs, unauthorized users may gain access to systems or data, increasing risk of data breaches and insider threats.

compensating_control_1.name: Least Privilege
compensating_control_1.description: Mechanisms exist to utilize the concept of least privilege, allowing only authorized access to processes necessary to accomplish assigned tasks in accordance with organizational business functions.
compensating_control_1.justification: Least Privilege (IAC-21) provides access control enforcement that compensates for the absence of Use of Privileged Utility Programs (IAC-20.3) by restricting system and data access to authorized users through alternative identity and access mechanisms. Given the technology-focused nature of this control, this compensating control targets the same underlying risk objective through an alternative approach, helping to maintain an acceptable level of residual risk until the primary control can be implemented.

compensating_control_2.name: Access Enforcement
compensating_control_2.description: Mechanisms exist to enforce Logical Access Control (LAC) permissions that conform to the principle of "least privilege."
compensating_control_2.justification: Access Enforcement (IAC-20) provides access control enforcement that compensates for the absence of Use of Privileged Utility Programs (IAC-20.3) by restricting system and data access to authorized users through alternative identity and access mechanisms. Given the technology-focused nature of this control, this compensating control targets the same underlying risk objective through an alternative approach, helping to maintain an acceptable level of residual risk until the primary control can be implemented.
